name: Sai Teja Ragula

summary: AI Engineer with 5 plus years of software development experience and deep hands-on expertise building production AI applications using Large Language Models. Currently building LLM-powered features on Google Cloud Platform with Vertex AI and Gemini. Skilled in multi-model orchestration, RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, and prompt engineering. Strong full stack background with Java Spring Boot backend and React TypeScript frontend. Experienced deploying AI systems on GCP and AWS with Kubernetes and Docker.

aiAndLLMs: Multi-Model Orchestration — routing queries to the right model by complexity
Prompt Engineering — chain-of-thought, few-shot, system prompts, output formatting
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) — Pinecone, embeddings, semantic search

AI Agents — multi-step reasoning, memory, planning

experience.title: AI / Software Engineer
experience.description: I work for a major US telecom provider as a Java Full Stack Developer on their enterprise team, building LLM-powered features and microservices. The project is Digital Deflection — a platform that deflects customer calls and messages to automated channels (IVR, SMS, Mobile App) to reduce cost and improve customer experience. The system automates troubleshooting, handles trouble tickets and service orders (new orders, change orders), coordinates across multiple systems of record, and sends real-time status updates to customers — technician arrivals, repair confirmations, appointment updates.
highlights: Built and maintained the Digital Deflection platform that automates customer interactions across IVR, SMS, and Mobile channels — reducing live agent dispatches and call volume
Integrated NLP with the Digital Deflection application for SMS communication — designed portals for automated troubleshooting flows and customer-facing SMS journeys
Coordinated with multiple systems of record to handle end-to-end Customer and Ticket journeys for trouble tickets, new service orders, and change orders

Implemented real-time digital communications: status updates to customers on technician arrivals, repair completions, and ticket status — improving customer experience and eliminating unnecessary dispatches
Understood business use case documents and implemented business rules based on requirements; provided technical and architectural leadership
Designed and configured Java application framework and implementation layers with proper class structure for Mobile App integration
Conducted component-level work estimation; assisted in project plan development, execution, and change control
Participated in Agile ceremonies — sprint planning, sprint demos, and retrospectives
Provided System Design documentation, Architecture Support, and Production support
Developed RESTful APIs using Spring Boot, Spring MVC, Spring IOC, and Spring Security; integrated with Apache Kafka for asynchronous messaging
Integrated Spring Boot microservices with Apache Kafka and deployed in Docker containers on AWS EC2; managed AWS IAM, Lambda, S3, Glue, and CloudWatch
Built React TypeScript frontend features including real-time dashboards, SPA components using React Hooks, React-Router, and Redux
Implemented CI/CD pipelines using Docker, Jenkins, and GitLab; deployed microservices on Kubernetes
Implemented caching with Redis; PL/SQL for Oracle database; migrations to DB2; Splunk for centralized log analysis and dashboards
Utilized Kafka with Spark Streaming for real-time data processing, storing results in PostgreSQL
Achieved 90 percent plus code coverage through JUnit and Mockito unit and integration tests

experience.title: Software Engineer

Upgraded application code from Java 8 to Java 17, ensuring better performance and compliance with modern standards

Transformed and optimized legacy utility files; centralized secret key storage through Fortanix across all environments — reduced code maintenance and enhanced security
Resolved 98 percent of bugs on time, ensuring smooth project delivery
Directed multifaceted teams through full development and production deployment lifecycle, ensuring flawless delivery
Actively participated in all SDLC phases: requirement gathering, design, development, unit testing, QA, UAT, and production deployment
Regularly upgraded dependencies each quarter to eliminate vulnerabilities and improve code security

experience.title: Software Engineer

projects.name: Wealthix — AI-Powered Financial Intelligence Platform
projects.description: End-to-end AI financial platform. Built a Hybrid Router pattern that dynamically selects between Gemini 1.5 and Claude based on query type — fast financial lookups go to Gemini Flash, complex reasoning like tax optimization goes to Claude. Implemented a full RAG pipeline: PDF financial documents ingested, chunked, embedded with OpenAI embeddings, stored in Pinecone, retrieved by semantic similarity at query time. Deployed on AWS with load balancing and monitoring.
highlights: Multi-model orchestration: cost-optimized routing between Gemini Flash and Claude based on prompt complexity score
RAG pipeline end to end: ingest, chunk, embed, upsert, retrieve, inject into LLM context
Plaid integration for real bank account data — AI reasons over actual transaction history
Stripe payment integration with AI-generated invoice summaries

projects.name: AI Financial Co-Pilot — Automated Dispute Engine
projects.description: Agentic AI system for automated financial dispute resolution. The AI reads dispute documents using Claude 3.5, extracts key claims, cross-references transaction records, drafts resolution letters, and routes edge cases to human review. Built prompt engineering pipelines with structured output constraints so Claude always returns valid JSON that maps to dispute categories.
highlights: Claude 3.5 for document analysis — structured output with JSON schema enforcement
Agentic routing: AI decides whether to auto-resolve or escalate to human
Prompt pipeline with few-shot examples for consistent dispute classification
Real-time dashboard showing dispute status, AI confidence scores, and resolution timeline

projects.name: AI Call Screener — Voice AI Agent
projects.description: Real-time voice AI agent that intercepts recruiter phone calls and answers as me. Twilio Media Streams provides μ-law 8kHz audio via WebSocket. Deepgram nova-3 does streaming STT. Claude claude-sonnet-4-5 reasons from my resume context with RAG (Pinecone) for self-improvement after each call. ElevenLabs TTS generates the voice response. The system detects interruptions using AtomicReference and stops mid-sentence — exactly like a real person would. Conference bridge architecture enables seamless live call handoff between AI and human.
highlights: Real-time audio pipeline: Twilio WebSocket → Deepgram STT → Claude reasoning → ElevenLabs TTS → Twilio
Interrupt detection: AtomicReference tracks mid-speech caller interruptions, AI stops at sentence boundary
Conference bridge: Twilio conference rooms allow seamless AI ↔ human call handoff with no audio gap
Self-training: each call transcript ingested into Pinecone RAG so AI improves over time
Java virtual threads for non-blocking AI pipeline — each call runs concurrently without blocking

commonInterviewAnswers.tellMeAboutYourself: I am an AI and Full Stack Engineer with over 5 years of experience. I build LLM-powered features, RAG pipelines, and agentic workflows. I am currently open to new opportunities.
commonInterviewAnswers.whyLeavingCurrentJob: I am looking for a role that is even more AI-focused, ideally where building AI systems is the core product.
commonInterviewAnswers.biggestStrength: I can take an AI concept from prototype to production end to end — the LLM logic, the backend engineering, the deployment. Most people are strong in one area, I am comfortable across the full stack.
commonInterviewAnswers.biggestWeakness: I sometimes go too deep on a problem when a simpler approach would work. I am getting better at asking what the minimum viable solution is first.
commonInterviewAnswers.whereDoYouSeeYourselfIn5Years: Leading AI architecture at a larger scale — setting the design for how LLMs are integrated across an entire product.
commonInterviewAnswers.salaryExpectation: Market competitive — open to discussion based on the role and scope.
commonInterviewAnswers.whyShouldWeHireYou: I bring production experience with LLM applications, a strong Java full-stack background, and I can operate independently across the entire delivery pipeline.
